content_types.name: career_personal_milestone
content_types.definition: A post announcing a significant personal or professional life event, such as a new job, promotion, marriage, or other achievement. The tone is celebratory and emotionally driven rather than instructional or data-heavy.
inclusion_criteria: First-person announcement of a life or career event
Celebratory or grateful tone with thanks to supporters
Short word count with no instructional content or data
No call to action beyond acknowledgment or congratulations
Subject is the author's own experience, not an external entity

content_types.name: company_milestone_announcement
content_types.definition: A post announcing any significant company achievement by an insider, including funding rounds, product launches, award recognitions, major partnerships where the author's company is the primary subject, employee programs, and expansion announcements. Credibility comes from at least one quantitative proof point, named investors, customer logos, or award bodies. The post is written from an insider perspective with a celebratory framing.
inclusion_criteria: Announces a specific company achievement such as funding, launch, award, expansion, or employee program
Includes at least one quantitative metric, named investor, customer logo, or award body as a credibility signal
Written from the perspective of a founder, executive, or official company voice
Celebratory framing focused on the author's own organization as the primary subject
May include investor names, customer counts, revenue figures, or award details as proof points

content_types.name: retrospective_lessons
content_types.definition: A post that looks back on a completed period, project, or milestone and extracts numbered or structured lessons for an audience. Combines a milestone hook with a structured retrospective body.
inclusion_criteria: References a completed phase, milestone, or time period as the anchor
Body contains numbered or explicitly sequenced takeaways
Each lesson includes a brief explanatory narrative
Author writes from direct experience rather than external research
Intended to be actionable or instructive for peers in similar situations

content_types.name: personal_narrative_reflection
content_types.definition: A first-person post in which the author processes an experience, emotion, or challenge through storytelling. The post may include loosely structured takeaways but lacks a formal milestone anchor or strictly sequential lessons. The tone is reflective and honest rather than celebratory or instructional.
inclusion_criteria: Written entirely in first person from lived experience
Primary mode is narrative rather than enumeration or structured listing
May include a soft practical conclusion but not a numbered framework
Tone is introspective, candid, or vulnerable rather than celebratory
No product or company promotion as the main purpose

content_types.name: opinion_hot_take
content_types.definition: A post centered on a single strong opinion, provocative claim, or contrarian argument. The post prioritizes persuasion and reaction over information delivery, often using a striking contrast or rhetorical question as the hook.
inclusion_criteria: Opens with a bold, debatable claim or provocative framing
Minimal data presented; argument is driven by reasoning or analogy
Author positions themselves against a common assumption or behavior
Rhetorical questions or direct challenges to the audience are present
Short to medium length with a punchy, memorable core statement

content_types.name: educational_explainer
content_types.definition: A post that teaches a concept, framework, or technical topic to an audience. It uses structured comparisons, step-by-step breakdowns, or research citations to build understanding rather than to promote or announce.
inclusion_criteria: Primary goal is audience comprehension of a concept or methodology
Uses contrast, comparison, or sequential explanation as a structural device
References external research, papers, or authoritative sources
No product or personal promotion embedded as the main purpose
Ends with a learning prompt or open question to the audience

content_types.name: data_research_insight
content_types.definition: A post that presents original or curated quantitative findings, research observations, or ranked data to surface a non-obvious insight. The post's credibility rests on the data rather than on personal authority alone.
inclusion_criteria: Contains at least one specific statistic, percentage, or ranked data set
References a research methodology, sample, or data source
Frames the data around a finding or trend rather than pure announcement
May segment data across dimensions such as company size or time period
Author positions themselves as analyst or curator of the evidence

content_types.name: product_feature_pitch
content_types.definition: A post that announces, explains, or promotes a specific product or set of features, either from the product creator or an enthusiast reviewer. The post enumerates capabilities with the intent to drive adoption or interest.
inclusion_criteria: Enumerates three or more distinct product features or capabilities
Language is promotional or launch-focused with superlatives or comparisons to alternatives
May include step-by-step instructions or a 'how it works' section
Closes with a URL, sign-up prompt, or call to try the product
May reference integrations, pricing tiers, or competitor positioning

content_types.name: partnership_announcement
content_types.definition: A post announcing a formal collaboration, integration, or joint go-to-market effort between two or more named organizations. The post explains the rationale for the partnership, the combined value proposition, and how customers or users benefit.
inclusion_criteria: Names two or more distinct organizations as co-parties to the collaboration
Articulates complementary or additive value resulting from the combination
May include a quote from a leader at one or both organizations
Often includes a joint call to action such as a booth, URL, or product trial
Written from the perspective of one party but acknowledges the other as an equal contributor

content_types.name: event_promotion_announcement
content_types.definition: A post promoting an upcoming in-person or virtual event, conference, or session. It includes actionable logistics such as date, location, and registration details, with a clear call to attend.
inclusion_criteria: Specifies a named event with a date, time, or location
Includes a registration link, RSVP prompt, or attendance instruction
Names co-hosts, speakers, or organizing entities
Written before the event occurs with forward-looking urgency
May list multiple sessions or sub-events within a broader program

content_types.name: in_progress_event_update
content_types.definition: A post published during an ongoing multi-day event that combines a recap of activities already completed with a forward-looking teaser for upcoming sessions. The author is typically an organizer or active participant bridging what has happened and what is still to come.
inclusion_criteria: References an event that has started but not yet concluded
Includes at least one completed activity, session, or moment summary
Includes at least one upcoming activity or call to attend future sessions
Tone is energetic and promotional rather than purely retrospective
May name speakers, sponsors, or participants from both past and future sessions

content_types.name: event_community_recap
content_types.definition: A post reflecting on a completed event or community gathering, highlighting who attended, what was discussed, and the value created. The tone is warm and relationship-focused, often naming participants.
inclusion_criteria: References an event that has already occurred in its entirety
Names specific attendees, speakers, or participating companies
Tone is celebratory, grateful, or reflective rather than instructional
May include timestamps, agenda items, or a chronological narrative
Often closes with a thank-you or signals future repetition of the event

content_types.name: industry_news_commentary
content_types.definition: A post in which the author summarizes or reacts to an external news story, industry development, or third-party announcement. The author is not the subject of the news and adds editorial framing or interpretation.
inclusion_criteria: Subject of the post is an external company, person, or event the author did not create
Author adds interpretive commentary or a closing editorial observation
References specific figures, quotes, or facts from the external story
May link to an external article or source
Tone ranges from neutral curation to strong opinion about the news

content_types.name: link_share_minimal_commentary
content_types.definition: A post whose primary function is to surface an external URL with minimal or no original editorial content. The author acts as a distributor or amplifier rather than a commentator or analyst.
inclusion_criteria: Original authored text is under 30 words or consists only of a headline restatement
A URL is the functional payload of the post
Hashtags may be present but add no analytical or interpretive value
No argument, question, or original opinion is introduced by the author
Often posted by media brands, aggregators, or newsletter accounts

content_types.name: narrative_scenario_case_study
content_types.definition: A post that uses a story, fictional scenario, or real customer example to illustrate a risk, opportunity, or product benefit. The narrative structure is the primary vehicle for the argument or proof.
inclusion_criteria: Uses a story arc with a setup, complication, and resolution or lesson
May be a fictional but realistic scenario or a named real-world example
Outcome or moral of the story supports a product, methodology, or opinion
Contains specific detail such as names, amounts, or dialogue to build credibility
Story functions as evidence rather than mere illustration

content_types.name: recruitment_job_posting
content_types.definition: A post whose primary purpose is to attract candidates for an open role at a company. It names the position, team, location, and application method without embedding significant instructional or opinion content.
inclusion_criteria: Names a specific open role or category of roles being hired
Includes team name, company name, or location information
Contains an application link or instruction on how to apply
Short word count focused on attracting candidates rather than sharing insight
No extended narrative or opinion; transactional and informational in structure

visual_modalities.name: multi_stat_panel
visual_modalities.definition: A grid or row of labeled statistic tiles, each displaying a headline number with a short descriptor. Used when a post contains three or more distinct quantitative proof points that together tell a story of scale or achievement.
inclusion_criteria: Post contains three or more distinct numeric metrics or milestone figures
Metrics are parallel in nature and reinforce a single overarching claim
Content is announcement, funding, or data-insight oriented
Each metric can stand alone with a two-to-four word label
visual_modalities.renderability_spec: CSS grid with 2-4 columns of stat tiles. Each tile has a large bold number in a brand accent color at 32-48px, a short descriptor label below in 12-14px, and a light border or background tint to separate tiles. Responsive to single-column on narrow widths. Optional header row with the announcement title spanning full width.

visual_modalities.name: numbered_list_graphic
visual_modalities.definition: A vertically stacked series of numbered rows, each containing a short title and one-line description. Used when post content is structured as a sequential list of lessons, steps, bets, or takeaways.
inclusion_criteria: Post contains three or more explicitly numbered or sequenced items
Each item has a short title and brief explanatory note
Content is retrospective, instructional, or feature-listing in nature
Order of items is meaningful or carries narrative progression
visual_modalities.renderability_spec: Vertical flexbox column of row cards. Each row has a large bold number on the left in a circle or square badge, a title in 16-18px bold, and a subtitle in 13px regular weight. Alternating light background tints or left-border accents differentiate rows. Header block at top with overall title. Max width 640px.

visual_modalities.name: feature_list_graphic
visual_modalities.definition: A structured list of product features or capabilities, each represented as a labeled row with an optional icon or short descriptor. Used for product announcements, pitches, and reviews where multiple discrete capabilities need to be communicated.
inclusion_criteria: Post enumerates four or more distinct product features or specifications
Content is product announcement, pitch, or enthusiast review
Features are discrete and parallel rather than sequential steps
Post is promotional in tone with a call to try or learn more
visual_modalities.renderability_spec: Vertical list of feature rows inside a branded card container. Each row has an icon placeholder on the left (CSS shape or emoji), a bold feature name, and a short descriptor in regular weight. Divider lines between rows. Header block with product name and tagline. Optional badge or label for new or key features. Max width 600px.

visual_modalities.name: comparison_table
visual_modalities.definition: A two-column or multi-column table that contrasts two or more approaches, options, or states across several dimensions. Used for educational explainers and hot takes that hinge on a clear before-and-after or either-or contrast.
inclusion_criteria: Post explicitly contrasts two or more approaches, systems, or conditions
Three or more comparison dimensions exist that can become table rows
Content is educational, analytical, or argument-driven
The contrast itself is the main persuasive or instructional device
visual_modalities.renderability_spec: HTML table with a sticky header row naming each option. Each subsequent row represents one comparison dimension with a left-side label column and one cell per option. Background tinting alternates rows for readability. One column can be highlighted with a brand color border to indicate a preferred option. Max width 680px with responsive horizontal scroll.

visual_modalities.name: timeline_graphic
visual_modalities.definition: A chronologically ordered visual that plots events, milestones, or moments along a horizontal or vertical axis. Used for personal stories, event recaps, and expansion announcements with explicit time or sequence markers.
inclusion_criteria: Post contains three or more time-stamped or sequenced events
Chronological order is meaningful to the narrative or argument
Content is a personal story, event recap, or expansion roadmap
Each event has a short label that can stand alone in a node
visual_modalities.renderability_spec: Vertical or horizontal flexbox track with a central line drawn using a CSS border. Each node is a circle element positioned along the line with a year or time label and a short event description beside it. Alternating left-right layout for vertical timelines to avoid crowding. Future nodes styled with dashed borders or muted color. Max width 640px.

visual_modalities.name: event_details_card
visual_modalities.definition: A structured card that surfaces the key logistics of an upcoming event including name, date, time, location, hosts, and a call to action. Designed to communicate all actionable information at a glance.
inclusion_criteria: Post promotes a specific upcoming event with a defined date and location
Multiple logistical details exist that benefit from structured layout
A clear call to action such as RSVP or register is present
Content is transactional and forward-looking rather than reflective
visual_modalities.renderability_spec: Card container with a bold header block for event name and tagline. Below the header, a two-column detail grid with icon-label pairs for date, time, venue, and hosts. A prominent CTA button styled with brand color at the bottom. Optional co-host name badges rendered as pill elements. Max width 560px with rounded corners and a drop shadow.

visual_modalities.name: checklist_graphic
visual_modalities.definition: A visual list of items each marked with a pass, fail, or status indicator. Used for compliance scenarios, gap analyses, or requirement checklists where the presence or absence of each item is the core message.
inclusion_criteria: Post contains a list of requirements, criteria, or items that can be checked or evaluated
Pass-fail or present-absent status is meaningful for each item
Content is compliance, audit, risk, or self-assessment oriented
The gap between required and actual state is the rhetorical engine of the post
visual_modalities.renderability_spec: Vertical list of checklist rows. Each row has a status indicator on the left rendered as a CSS circle with a checkmark, X, or warning symbol using Unicode or CSS content. Item label in 14-16px with a strikethrough or muted style for failed items. Optional status badge on the right such as 'Missing' or 'Incomplete' rendered as a pill. Container has a light background with a header row. Max width 600px.

visual_modalities.name: two_column_process_diagram
visual_modalities.definition: A side-by-side or stacked two-panel layout that represents two distinct phases, components, or approaches of a framework or methodology. Used for structured announcements of blueprints, strategies, or two-part systems.
inclusion_criteria: Post describes exactly two phases, components, or contrasting approaches
Each component has its own sub-list of features or functions
Content is a framework announcement, methodology reveal, or structured explainer
The two parts are meant to be understood as complementary or sequential
visual_modalities.renderability_spec: Two-column CSS grid or flexbox row, each column rendered as a card with a bold phase label at the top and a bullet list of functions below. A connector element such as an arrow or numbered badge between columns signals direction or sequence. Both columns share equal width with consistent padding. Optional header spanning both columns for the overall framework name. Max width 700px.

visual_modalities.name: ranked_list_graphic
visual_modalities.definition: A vertically ordered list where items are sorted by a quantitative value and each row displays the rank, label, and corresponding metric. Used for data insight posts built around comparative rankings.
inclusion_criteria: Post contains five or more items that are explicitly or implicitly ranked by a numeric value
Rank order is central to the insight or argument being made
Content is data-driven with each item having a consistent comparable metric
Visual communication of relative magnitude adds meaningful interpretive value
visual_modalities.renderability_spec: Vertical ordered list with a rank number column on the far left, an entity name column in the center, and a metric value column on the right. A horizontal bar of variable width behind each row can encode relative magnitude using CSS width percentages. Alternating row background tints improve readability. Header row labels each column. Top-ranked rows can use a brand accent color for emphasis. Max width 660px.

visual_modalities.name: pull_quote_card
visual_modalities.definition: A card featuring a single powerful sentence or short statement with large typographic emphasis. Used for one-liner hot takes, bold declarations, and punchy standalone messages.
inclusion_criteria: Post centers on a single memorable sentence or phrase
No attribution to a specific named person is present
Content is a hot take, bold declaration, or punchy one-liner
Minimal supporting context - the statement stands alone
visual_modalities.renderability_spec: Centered flex container with the statement in bold display font at 28-40px. No attribution line. Background uses a solid brand color or gradient. Optional large decorative quotation marks. Max width 600px with generous padding.

visual_modalities.name: headline_subtext_card
visual_modalities.definition: A card with a prominent title or headline followed by 1-2 supporting context lines below. Used for announcements, news summaries, and key takeaways that need brief elaboration.
inclusion_criteria: Post has a clear headline or title-worthy statement
One or two supporting lines provide context or elaboration
Content is an announcement, news summary, or key takeaway
The structure is hierarchical: primary message + secondary context
visual_modalities.renderability_spec: Vertical flex container. Headline in bold 24-32px at top. Subtext in regular 14-16px below with muted color. Optional category label or date badge above headline. Thin accent line separating headline from subtext. Max width 600px.

visual_modalities.name: attribution_quote_card
visual_modalities.definition: A quote or statement explicitly attributed to a specific person with their name and optionally their role or organization. Used for testimonials, lessons from named individuals, and "someone said X" patterns.
inclusion_criteria: Post contains a quote or statement attributed to a named person
Attribution includes at least a name, often with role or company
The quoted person may or may not be the post author
Content is a testimonial, lesson, or notable statement from a specific individual
visual_modalities.renderability_spec: Centered flex container with large blockquote in serif or bold font at 24-36px. Attribution line below with name in bold and role in muted smaller type. Large decorative quotation mark in accent color. Optional avatar placeholder circle. Max width 600px.

visual_modalities.name: bold_statement_card
visual_modalities.definition: A centered, minimal card with strong typographic treatment and no attribution. Used for opinions, provocative claims, motivational statements, and declarations where the message itself is the focus.
inclusion_criteria: Post is centered on a strong opinion, provocative claim, or motivational statement
No specific person is quoted or attributed
The message is designed for impact and shareability
Minimal or no supporting context beyond the core statement
visual_modalities.renderability_spec: Full-width centered card with statement in bold uppercase or display font at 32-48px. Minimal decoration. High contrast background. No attribution line. Optional brand mark or icon. Max width 600px with large vertical padding.

visual_modalities.name: key_takeaway_card
visual_modalities.definition: A card presenting a distilled insight or lesson with brief supporting context. Used for summaries of longer narratives, extracted wisdom, and condensed learnings that benefit from a small explanatory note.
inclusion_criteria: Post contains a distilled insight, lesson, or key point
Brief supporting context or explanation accompanies the main takeaway
Content is a summary, extracted wisdom, or condensed learning
The takeaway is positioned as a practical or actionable insight
visual_modalities.renderability_spec: Card with a "KEY TAKEAWAY" or "INSIGHT" label badge at top. Main insight in bold 20-28px. Supporting context in regular 14px below with a subtle background tint. Left accent border in brand color. Max width 600px.
